author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
verses:
vṛndāvane viharatoriha kelikuñje
matta⋅dvipa⋅pravara⋅kautuka⋅vibhrameṇa
sandarśayasva juvayorvadanāravinda⋅
dvandvaṁ vidhehi mayi devi! kṛpāṁ prasīda

hā devi! kāku⋅bhara⋅gadgada⋅jāḍya vācā
jāce nipatya bhuvi daṇḍavadudbhaṭārtiḥ
asya prasādamabudhasya janasya kṛtvā
gāndharvike! nijagaṇe gaṇanāṁ vidhehi

śyāme! ramā⋅ramaṇa⋅sundaratā⋅variṣṭha⋅
saundarya⋅mohita⋅samasta⋅jagajjanasya
śyāmasya vāma⋅bhuja⋅baddhatanuṁ kadāhaṁ
tvāmindirā⋅virala⋅rūpa⋅bharāṁ bhajāmi?

tvāṁ pracchadena mudira⋅cchavinā pidhāya
mañjīra⋅mukta⋅caraṇāñca vidhāya devi!
kuñje vrajendra⋅tanayena virājamāne
naktaṁ kadā pramuditāmabhisārayiṣye?

kuñje prasūna⋅kula⋅kalpita⋅keli⋅talpe
saṁviṣṭayor⋅madhura⋅narma⋅vilāsa⋅bhājoḥ
loka⋅trayābharaṇayoścaraṇāmbujāni
samvāhayiṣyati kadā juvayorjano‛yam?

tvat⋅kuṇḍa⋅rodhasi vilāsa⋅pariśrameṇa
svedāmbu⋅cumbi⋅vadanāmburuha⋅śriyau vām
vṛndāvaneśvari! kadā taru-mūla-bhājau
samvījayāmi camarī⋅caya⋅cāmareṇa?

līnāṁ nikuñja⋅kuhare bhavatīṁ mukunde
citraiva sūcitavatī rucirākṣi! nāham
bhugnāṁ bhruvaṁ na racayeti mṛṣā⋅ruṣāṁ tvā⋅
magre vrajendra⋅tanayasya kadā nu neṣye?

vāgyuddha⋅keli⋅kutuke vraja⋅rāja⋅sūnuṁ
jitvonmadāmadhika⋅darpa⋅vikāsi⋅jalpām
phullābhir⋅ālibhir⋅analpam⋅udīryamāṇa⋅
stotrāṁ kadā nu bhavatīmavalokayiṣye?

jaḥ ko‛pi suṣṭhu vṛṣabhānu⋅kumārikāyāḥ
samprārthanāṣṭakam⋅idaṁ paṭhati prapannaḥ
sā preyasā saha sametya dhṛta⋅pramodā
tatra prasāda⋅laharīmurarī⋅karoti
en_translation:
O Devi! Be pleased! Bestow mercy upon me! Show me the pair of lotus faces of You two sporting here in the play-groves of Vṛndāvana with the playful movements of the best of intoxicated elephants.

Alas, O Devi! With a voice choked with the weight of piteous words, falling on the ground like a stick, I beg with intense distress: making this ignorant person an object of Your mercy, O Gāndharvikā, count me among Your own people.

O Śyāmā! When will I worship You, whose body is bound by the left arm of Śyāma, who has enchanted all the people of the universe with a beauty superior to the beauty of the Husband of Ramā (Viṣṇu), and who possesses a wealth of form rare even for Indirā?

O Devi! Covering You with a cloth of the color of a cloud and removing the anklets from Your feet, when will I joyfully make You go on a tryst at night in the bower where the Son of the King of Vraja is shining?

When will this person massage the lotus feet of You two, the ornaments of the three worlds, who are seated on a play-bed made of multitudes of flowers in the bower and enjoying sweet, joking pastimes?

O Queen of Vṛndāvana! When will I fan You two with a yak-tail whisk, seated at the root of a tree on the bank of Your Kuṇḍa, Your lotus faces kissing the drops of sweat from the fatigue of pastimes?

O beautiful-eyed one! You are hiding in the hollow of a bower with Mukunda. "Citra herself indicated it; not I!"—when will I lead You, who are thus feigning anger with knitted eyebrows, before the Son of the King of Vraja?

When will I see You, having conquered the Son of the King of Vraja in the curiosity of a war of words, intoxicated with pride, with speech expanding with great arrogance, being praised profusely by the blooming friends?

Whoever surrenders and recites this prayer of eight verses to the Daughter of Vṛṣabhānu nicely, She, meeting with Her Beloved and holding joy, accepts a wave of mercy towards him.
